title: Update to use shared configfile implementation
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?

<!--
Please label this pull request according to what type of issue you are
addressing, especially if this is a release targeted pull request.

Uncomment only one `/kind <>` line, hit enter to put that in a new line, and
remove leading whitespace from that line:
-->

/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Update to use shared configfile implementation
This updates Buildah to use the new configfile api from
container-libs https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/611.

#### How to verify it

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

<!--
Automatically closes linked issue when PR is merged.
Uncomment the following comment block and include the issue
number or None on one line.
Usage: `Fixes #<issue number>`, or `Fixes (paste link of issue)`, or `None`.
-->

<!--
Fixes #
or
None
-->

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note

```

comments.body: (I have added “No new tests” to let the CI run, and switched the PR to draft, correspondingly. If this PR does end up making changes to the repo, please consider removing the label.)

I've checked the test failures and both `not ok 858 push with authfile` and `not ok 570 chroot with overlay root` fail even without this patch applied. I therefore think it is not caused by any change in the container-libs

> I've checked the test failures and both `not ok 858 push with authfile`

`Error: preparing instances for copy: requested operation filtered out all platforms and would create an empty image` should be resolved by using a newer c/image — please rebase your PR.

>  and `not ok 570 chroot with overlay root` fail 

`# Error: open /root/.config/containers/policy.json: permission denied`

Really, there is no relationship to the c/image `policy.json` work here?!

> even without this patch applied.

To confirm, is that with the latest `container-libs`, or with the base of the 711 PR (almost a month old now)?

---

If we have CI failing on current `main`, I think that’s an ~urgent situation and there should be $someone working on that or at least an issue filed. Admittedly I do see some failures on `main`, but not those, and a recent https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/41c29785613693fd6cd2f5a68e4c27b3d89db559 did pass all tests.

So I’d expect _some_ action to be taken based on those failures — either initiating a fix process on `main`, or changing this testing PR.

> > and `not ok 570 chroot with overlay root` fail
> 
> `# Error: open /root/.config/containers/policy.json: permission denied`
> 
> Really, there is no relationship to the c/image `policy.json` work here?!
> 
> > even without this patch applied.
> 
> To confirm, is that with the latest `container-libs`, or with the base of the 711 PR (almost a month old now)?
> 


This is almost certainly caused by this PR, other config files were no different https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6714/changes/12bbaf596e70297ec80fc64c679d66ebb075540a

This buildah test sets up a custom chroot without access to /root, so this need a env var or cli level overwrite, i.e. may buildah test seem to do that with `$WITH_POLICY_JSON` already
OK, lesson learned, `make test-integration` does not run `make` automatically. I've been testing this with wrong binary :-(.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

Well, the `push.bats` part of https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6787/changes/796009cf9401c64fc1d0d31fd6e9464e4074c0f5#diff-bd63e6752b89b04d7ed374634cb8adbb540561d29c2a46baf4bab11edaab3235 looks weird.

It is something which should have been fixed by @mtrmac 's https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/713. I see the same issues with latest container-libs in the podman PR: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28508.

It's probably yet another issue on my side, I will investigate this more...

> Well, the `push.bats` part of [796009c#diff-bd63e6752b89b04d7ed374634cb8adbb540561d29c2a46baf4bab11edaab3235](https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/796009cf9401c64fc1d0d31fd6e9464e4074c0f5#diff-bd63e6752b89b04d7ed374634cb8adbb540561d29c2a46baf4bab11edaab3235) looks weird.
> 
> It is something which should have been fixed by @mtrmac 's [containers/container-libs#713](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/713). I see the same issues with latest container-libs in the podman PR: [containers/podman#28508](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28508).
> 
> It's probably yet another issue on my side, I will investigate this more...

Yeah, I updated to latest image commit after I did that fix. Should be OK now :-).
Please add some proper commit message for the fix tests commit when you rebase here and pick up the changes from main
